ai_disclosure: Items in this week were drafted from hand-written editorial templates for end-to-end pipeline testing. No AI rewriting was applied at generation time.

items.headline: D7 visa renewals slow at the SEF in May.
items.lede: Two-month wait for the in-person interview, by appointment only.
items.body: Service desks at the Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras have been booked through July for D7 renewals. Online pre-interviews are accepted only for second-time renewals. New applicants should expect three desk visits over four months.
items.quote: Three desk visits, four months. Be early.

items.headline: NHR 2.0 frozen at the same rate through 2027.
items.lede: The 20 % flat for new highly-qualified arrivals stays.
items.body: AT (Autoridade Tributária) confirmed in writing that NHR 2.0, the post-2024 successor to NHR, will keep its 20 % flat band for IFICI-classified arrivals through fiscal year 2027.
items.quote: No further reform announced this term.

items.headline: Câmara closes Praça do Comércio fountain through August.
items.lede: Restoration. Pigeons unaffected.
items.body: The fountain at the south end of Praça do Comércio will be drained and resealed during the summer. Pedestrian access to the square is unchanged. Editor expects the seagulls to redistribute to Cais do Sodré.

items.headline: F-2-R digital nomad visa issuance steady at 200/month.
items.lede: No backlog reported at HiKorea.
items.body: The Ministry of Justice continues issuing the F-2-R digital nomad visa at the post-launch monthly cap. Applicants reporting 7–10 working days from submission to approval. The HiKorea online portal accepts the entire flow without an in-person visit.

items.headline: NTS confirms 183-day rule on calendar year basis.
items.lede: No proration for partial-year arrivals in 2026.
items.body: A reader-submitted query to the National Tax Service was answered in writing this week: residency for 2026 is established by aggregate days inside Korea between January 1 and December 31, with no proration for arrival mid-year.

items.headline: Hannam-dong cafe opens 24-hour shift through Q3.
items.lede: Three outlets per table. No noise after 22h00.
items.body: A long-standing Hannam-dong reading café announced a summer pilot of 24-hour operation through the end of September. Staff confirm three power outlets per table, headphones expected after 22h00. Editor visited twice this month.

items.headline: Designated Activities visa retains six-month default.
items.lede: No further extension granted by Cabinet Office in May.
items.body: The Cabinet Office's review of the Designated Activities visa (the de facto digital-nomad track in Japan) confirmed the six-month default for fiscal 2026. Renewals require a fresh income proof and an exit-and-return.

items.headline: NTA confirms one-year rule starts on entry, not on registration.
items.lede: A reader sent a NTA letter; we publish the answer.
items.body: A reader who registered late at their ward office asked whether their tax-resident clock began on entry to Japan or on registration. The NTA replied: on entry, calculated by passport stamp and immigration record.

items.headline: Shimokitazawa kissaten reopens in old hand.
items.lede: Same coffee. Old chairs. New door.
items.body: A Shimokitazawa kissaten that had been closed for a year re-opened this week, run by the original owner's niece. Hand-drip pour-over, vinyl on the back wall, three small tables. Editor recommends arriving before 14h.

items.headline: Berlin Senat confirms split-year residency rules.
items.lede: Arrival mid-year? You may be a partial-year resident.
items.body: A clarification published this week reminds new arrivals that Germany applies split-year residency: from the day a habitual abode is established. The 183-day rule is supplementary, not exclusive.

items.headline: Freiberufler permit interviews moved to Tegel office.
items.lede: Mitte and Pankow appointments redirected.
items.body: Berlin's Ausländerbehörde redirected all Freiberufler in-person interviews to the Tegel branch starting May. Existing appointments at Mitte and Pankow are honoured but new ones are issued for Tegel only.

items.headline: Revenue Department clarifies remitted-income rule.
items.lede: Income earned in 2026 and remitted in 2027 is taxable.
items.body: A revenue ruling published this week reaffirms that foreign-sourced income earned by a Thai tax resident is taxable when remitted, regardless of the year earned. Remittance triggers the tax event.
items.source: กรมสรรพากร · Revenue Department

items.headline: DTV (Destination Thailand Visa) renewals open online.
items.lede: No more in-person at Chaeng Wattana.
items.body: The Thailand Bureau of Immigration opened DTV renewals on the e-extension portal in May. Renewal fee ฿10 000, validity stays five years, single-entry condition unchanged.

items.headline: Roma Norte rents soften 4 % year-on-year.
items.lede: Coliving operators cite oversupply.
items.body: Quarterly figures from one of the larger CDMX coliving operators show a 4 % drop in average monthly rent in Roma Norte and Condesa, attributed to a wave of post-2024 building completions.

items.headline: Temporary Resident visa hits 180-day clearance time.
items.lede: INM workload in DF (CDMX) spiked in March.
items.body: Applicants for the residente temporal visa via the CDMX INM office report processing times of 90–180 days, up from 30–60 in 2024. Applicants outside CDMX (Querétaro, Mérida) report normal turnaround.

items.headline: Indonesia E33G remote-worker visa fee unchanged.
items.lede: IDR 8.5M for the year. No bump in 2026.
items.body: The Directorate of Immigration confirmed in writing that the E33G visa fee will remain at IDR 8.5M for 2026 fiscal year. The visa is single-entry; multi-entry costs IDR 10.5M.

items.headline: Canggu council caps new villa permits.
items.lede: No new tourism-zoned licences through Q4.
items.body: The Canggu sub-district council passed a temporary cap on new tourism-zoned villa permits, citing road-traffic and water-supply pressure. Existing rentals continue.

items.headline: Visa-free 365 days unchanged for most passports.
items.lede: Reciprocity with KR, JP, EU steady.
items.body: The Georgian government re-confirmed its 365-day visa-free policy for citizens of 95 countries through 2027, including South Korea, Japan, and the Schengen 27. Continuous 365 days are not interruptable by short exits.

items.headline: Vake apartment rents tilt 6 % higher year-on-year.
items.lede: Demand from Russian and Iranian arrivals.
items.body: Rental data from a major Tbilisi listings site shows Vake one-bedroom averages up 6 % year-on-year, attributed to a continued inflow of long-stay residents from Russia and Iran. Old town rents are flat.

items.headline: AFIP confirms peso-blue ambiguity in nomad reporting.
items.lede: Foreign income converted at official rate, not blue.
items.body: The Argentine tax agency clarified in a public Q&A that foreign-source income earned by tax-resident nomads must be reported at the official ARS rate, regardless of the parallel-market premium. The blue-dollar gap is not honoured.

items.headline: Palermo Soho extends street-pedestrianisation through August.
items.lede: Friday–Sunday only. Extended through Q3.
items.body: Buenos Aires city government extended weekend pedestrianisation of three Palermo Soho streets through the end of August. Co-working operators in the area report a 12 % weekend foot-traffic increase.

items.headline: El Poblado tightens short-let registration.
items.lede: Owners must register by July or face fines.
items.body: The Medellín municipality's tourism office requires all short-stay rental operators in El Poblado to register on the city's short-let portal by July 1. Registration is free; non-registration carries a COP 2M fine.
items.source: Alcaldía de Medellín · Secretaría de Turismo

items.headline: Migración Colombia maintains 90-day visa-on-arrival.
items.lede: Extension to 180 still costs COP 200 000.
items.body: No change to the 90-day visa-on-arrival or the 90-day extension fee was announced in the Q2 review. The Migrante Digital permit (visa V) processing time stays at 30–45 days through Bogotá.

items.headline: Bairro Alto noise ordinance widens to weeknights.
items.lede: Outdoor music ends at 23h00 Mon–Thu starting June.
items.body: A new municipal noise ordinance extends the existing weekend cap to weeknights. Tasca-style outdoor patios in Bairro Alto and Bica must end amplified music by 23h00 from Monday to Thursday from June 1.

items.headline: D7 visa renewals slow at the SEF in May.
items.lede: Two-month wait for the in-person interview, by appointment only.
items.body: Service desks at the Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras have been booked through July for D7 renewals. Online pre-interviews are accepted only for second-time renewals. New applicants should expect three desk visits over four months.
items.quote: Three desk visits, four months. Be early.

items.body: AT (Autoridade Tributária) confirmed in writing that NHR 2.0, the post-2024 successor to NHR, will keep its 20 % flat band for IFICI-classified arrivals through fiscal year 2027.
items.quote: No further reform announced this term.

items.headline: AREX express drops to ₩9 000 with new card.
items.lede: A bargain even at the new fare.
items.body: The Airport Express commuter rail introduces a contactless tap-card valid for one year that prices the express service at ₩9 000 — down from ₩11 000 for one-off tickets. Tax-residency reminder: AREX days do not count toward your 183.

items.headline: F-2-R digital nomad visa issuance steady at 200/month.
